items.title: Russell Brand pleads not guilty to more rape and sexual assault charges
items.summary_auto: British comedian Russell Brand pleaded not guilty on Tuesday to new counts of rape and sexual assault, related to alleged incidents dating back to 2009.

items.title: Mexico made 'high-risk bet' in taking down cartel boss El Mencho
items.summary_auto: After tracking El Mencho’s lover to his lair, Mexican special forces launched an operation by air and land, leading to two shootouts that left the drug boss mortally wounded.

items.title: Family health teams urge Ontario to up pay for nurses, health workers to retain them
items.summary_auto: Ontario is working toward attaching everyone in the province to a primary care provider, but family health teams are warning that as the government has its sights set on that laudable goal, the foundation of primary care is crumbling.

items.title: Nearly 80% of Canadians oppose idea of Alberta leaving the nation: polling
items.summary_auto: Recent polling suggests a majority of Canadians — if they could — would block both Alberta and Quebec from separating from Canada.

items.title: U.S women's hockey team declines invitation to attend Trump's state of the union address
items.summary_auto: The U.S. women's hockey gold medal-winning team said it has to decline an invitation from President Donald Trump to attend his state of the union address on Tuesday night.

items.title: Trump gives his state of the union address tonight. Here's what to watch for
20 years after winning Olympic gold, Brad Gushue is preparing to say goodbye to curling
items.summary_auto: On the 20th anniversary of his gold medal performance at the 2006 Winter Olympics, Brad Gushue is looking back with a mix of pride and disbelief on a storied career now winding down.

items.title: 'Your heart feels like it's back in Ukraine': Sask. university student reflects on 4 years of war
items.summary_auto: University of Saskatchewan student Nikita Sereda remembers waking up in his apartment in Kharkiv, Ukraine, to the sounds of war on Feb. 24, 2022.

items.title: Snowy winter in N.S. pushing tiny saw-whet owls into more populated areas
items.summary_auto: Northern saw-whet owls are smaller than robins and can find it difficult to penetrate the snow to reach their usual prey.

items.title: How L.A.-based YouTube star LaurDIY started out as a kid crafter in St. Catharines, Ont.
items.summary_auto: With nearly 8.5 million followers on YouTube, LaurDIY is one of the most recognizable names in crafts and lifestyle content. The St. Catharines-born content creator made a recent visit to her hometown, prioritizing family and creativity without pressure.

items.title: Crown corp. alerted minister's office about U.S. artillery ammunition sale connected to Israel
items.summary_auto: A Crown corporation alerted Canada's international trade minister about the sale of artillery ammunition to the U.S., some of which was destined for Israel, CBC News has learned.

items.title: Pre-construction condo buyers face steep losses as Toronto prices slide
items.summary_auto: Homebuyers who gambled that buying a home before it was actually constructed could get them an advance foothold in an expensive housing market are discovering they have few options to avoid losing hundreds of thousands of dollars, in a market that is considerably less expensive.

items.title: 'Putin will not stop': Zelenskyy warns world on 4th anniversary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy delivered a stark message to the world on the fourth anniversary of Russia's relentless war against his country, warning allies not to turn away or become distracted by other conflicts, lest they be next to need help.

items.title: Missing snowmobilers found dead beneath frozen ice near Anahim Lake, B.C.
items.summary_auto: RCMP say frozen ice concealed the cracks indicating that a pair of missing snowmobilers from 100 Mile House had fallen through Charlotte Lake in the B.C. Interior.

items.title: Will El Mencho's cartel wage 'total war' against Mexico? | About That
items.summary_auto: Violence and shelter-in-place orders are rippling out across Mexico after the military killed a cartel leader commonly known as 'El Mencho.' Andrew Chang explains how Jalisco New Generation, arguably Mexico's most dangerous cartel, has been building a criminal empire for several years. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: 4 years of war in Ukraine: by the numbers
items.summary_auto: Russia's invasion of Ukraine four years ago launched Europe's biggest conflict since the Second World War, causing immense suffering for civilians and harrowing ordeals for soldiers while rewriting the post-Cold War security order.

items.title: Canadian Olympians land back at home after Milano Cortina Games
items.summary_auto: Canada's Olympic athletes began arriving home Monday greeted by friends, family and fans as they returned from the Milano Cortina 2026 Olympics.

items.title: U.S. military says strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in Caribbean Sea
items.summary_auto: The U.S. military said it killed three people Monday in a strike on an alleged drug-smuggling vessel in the Caribbean Sea as part of the Trump administration's months-long campaign against alleged traffickers.

items.title: B.C. health-science workers reach tentative contract, including 12% wage increase over 4 years
items.summary_auto: The union says the deal includes wage increases of 12 per cent over four years, the maximum possible under the government's balanced measures mandate for public sector workers.

items.title: 'I think we'll have enough space' in office for public servants 4 days a week: Treasury Board president
items.summary_auto: Shafqat Ali's Monday statement comes only days after the treasury board suggested the opposite to Radio-Canada.

items.title: Liberals accept Conservative proposals to limit 'immense' cabinet powers in budget bill
items.summary_auto: The federal Liberals have accepted amendments to the most controversial section of the Carney government's budget bill, effectively putting guardrails on new proposed powers that would allow the government to grant corporations the ability to bypass existing laws and regulations.

items.title: Toronto man donates $10 million to expand OCD treatment centre that changed his life
items.summary_auto: For decades, Brian Reeve has lived with obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). Seven years ago, he hit a breaking point when the time-consuming rituals began interfering with his daily life and ability to function. After seeking treatment at the Frederick W. Thompson Anxiety Disorders Centre, he is now in remission and giving back through a large donation.

items.title: Senate committee recommends removing broad immigration powers from border bill
items.summary_auto: Senators on the social affairs committee want to see immigration-related sections in the government's border bill, C-12, removed or significantly modified by the Senate national security committee.

items.title: Why removing Andrew from the line of succession is not as easy as it sounds
items.summary_auto: With the prime minister of Australia now offering his support to ensure the scandal-plagued Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor never becomes King, the prospect of a shortened line of succession could be moving closer to reality.

items.title: CONCACAF monitoring violence in Mexico as Champions Cup continues
items.summary_auto: Soccer's governing body in North America says it's closely monitoring ongoing violence in Mexico as its international tournament, the 27-club Champions Cup, continues.

items.title: Where Trump sees big 'ugly' windmills, Canada sees opportunity
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Trump calls wind power projects "ugly losers," and it's putting a deep chill on investments. But Canada stands to benefit as wind companies consider new prospects off the East Coast.

items.title: Federal government seeking input to develop men's and boys' health strategy
items.summary_auto: The federal government is launching a "national conversation" to inform a future strategy on men's and boys' health.

items.title: U.S. tells some of its diplomats and their families to leave Lebanon amid Iran tensions
items.summary_auto: The United States has ordered non-essential diplomats and their family members at the U.S. Embassy in Beirut to leave Lebanon, the State Department said Monday, as tensions over Iran rise with the threat of a potentially imminent military strike.

items.title: NDP's lone Quebec MP says he's considering jump to provincial politics
items.summary_auto: Quebec NDP MP Alexandre Boulerice said Monday that he's seriously considering running for the Québec Solidaire in this year's provincial election.

items.title: N.S. budget contains major cuts as province confronts $1.2B deficit
items.summary_auto: About 1,000 full-time equivalent jobs could come off the books this year.
